LanguageData.TITAN_Warlord_desc: One of the three legendary beasts. Age: Unknown (Ancient). Features: - Immortal - Aura: Blood Rage
LanguageData.TITAN_VoidWalker_desc: One of the three legendary beasts. Age: Unknown (Ancient). Features: - Immortal - Extreme dodge and shield - Aura: Void Veil
LanguageData.TITAN_Matriarch_desc: One of the three legendary beasts. Age: Unknown (Ancient). Features: - Immortal - Aura: Life Bloom
LanguageData.TITAN_Spark_desc: A strangely mutated boomrat. It is the only friend of "Prototype #0" in the lab. Features: [Phoenix Protocol] Upon receiving a fatal blow, it will not die but trigger a massive explosion and enter forced hibernation reboot. [Bond] Gains combat boost when near Prototype #0.
LanguageData.TITAN_Hunter_desc: Biological weapons bred by the Empire to retrieve "Project TITAN" test subjects. They were modified from warg genes with pain and fear removed, muscles permanently engorged in a berserk state. Their only directive: hunt #0.
LanguageData.TITAN_ThrumboPrototype_desc: The final product of the Empire's "Project TITAN". Its fur glows a faint gold, a symbol of royal genes. It possesses powerful self-repair abilities and cannot be truly killed (only knocked down). Features: - Periodically sheds [Golden Scale] - Attracts wild Thrumbo when adult - Active Ability: Titan Resonance
LanguageData.TITAN_MutatedGland_desc: An unstable biological gland extracted from Hunters. Though useless for normal synthesis, its bio-energy is highly valuable on the black market.
LanguageData.TITAN_Incense_desc: A bundle of special herbs and chemicals. When lit, it releases pheromones invisible to humans but detectable by specific royal beasts. This is the starting point of "Project TITAN". Usage: Select a colonist -> Right-click incense -> Choose "Light Incense".
LanguageData.TITAN_PrototypeScale_desc: A scale shed from Prototype #0, still glowing faintly golden with special pheromones. This pheromone has no effect on normal creatures, but seems to attract its eternal companion. Usage: Select a colonist -> Right-click scale -> Choose "Activate Signal".
LanguageData.TITAN_CrimsonKeratin_desc: Keratin left behind by War Trace when it departs. Its molecular arrangement holds the ultimate secrets of biological weapons. Analyze at Research Table to unlock [Destroyer Module] schematic.
LanguageData.TITAN_VoidDust_desc: Crystalline powder left by Void Walker, containing phase shield frequency data. Analyze at Research Table to unlock [Fortress Module] schematic.
LanguageData.TITAN_LifeResin_desc: Active resin left by Matriarch of Life, containing secrets of rapid cell regeneration. Analyze at Research Table to unlock [Walker Module] schematic.
LanguageData.TITAN_Implant_CrimsonHeart_desc: Crimson potion made using War Trace genes. [Usage]: Select Thrumbo -> Health -> Surgery -> Feed "Crimson Mutagen". Effect: Painless, massive melee damage boost.
LanguageData.TITAN_Implant_VoidMembrane_desc: Azure potion made using Void Walker genes. [Usage]: Select Thrumbo -> Health -> Surgery -> Feed "Void Mutagen". Effect: High armor, high dodge.
LanguageData.TITAN_Implant_VerdantSpine_desc: Emerald potion made using Matriarch of Life genes. [Usage]: Select Thrumbo -> Health -> Surgery -> Feed "Verdant Mutagen". Effect: Very high carry weight, rapid regeneration.
LanguageData.ResearchProject_TITAN_GeneEngineering_de: Understand the basic structure of Titan creatures, laying foundation for further research.
LanguageData.ResearchProject_TITAN_PlantTech_desc: By studying the seeds carried on Prototype #0's fur, gain the technology to plant Titan Trees and harvest the super ration Titan Tubers.
LanguageData.ResearchProject_TITAN_Crimson_desc: Analyze War Trace genetic data to develop mutagen that greatly enhances muscles and pain nerves.
LanguageData.ResearchProject_TITAN_Void_desc: Analyze Void Walker phase data to develop mutagen that generates micro phase deflection fields under the skin.
LanguageData.ResearchProject_TITAN_Verdant_desc: Analyze Matriarch of Life biological data to develop mutagen that greatly enhances metabolism and regeneration.
LanguageData.Hediff_TITAN_Implant_CrimsonHeart_desc: Enhanced heart function from genetic mutation.
LanguageData.Hediff_TITAN_Implant_VoidMembrane_desc: Subcutaneous phase field from genetic mutation.
LanguageData.Hediff_TITAN_Implant_VerdantSpine_desc: Enhanced spinal nerves from genetic mutation.
LanguageData.Hediff_TITAN_Bond_Spark_desc: Feeling beloved friend "Spark" nearby. The raging genes calm down from that warm core radiation.
LanguageData.Hediff_TITAN_Bond_Thrumbo_desc: Soul link with Prototype #0. As long as #0 is nearby, Spark gains supernatural reaction speed and regeneration.
LanguageData.Hediff_TITAN_Aura_Warlord_desc: Affected by War Trace's berserk aura.
LanguageData.Hediff_TITAN_Aura_VoidWalker_desc: Protected by Void Walker's phase shield.
LanguageData.Hediff_TITAN_WarlordSoothing_desc: Prototype #0's psionics are trying to calm its rage. When resonance reaches 3 layers, it will stop attacking.
LanguageData.Hediff_TITAN_SparkReboot_desc: Phoenix Protocol activated. Initiating emergency reorganization sequence.
LanguageData.Message_TITAN_CannotHandleGenes: {0} cannot withstand Titan genes and spits out the potion.
LanguageData.Command_TitanResonance_desc: Use Prototype #0's royal genes to release a resonance wave. [Cooldown: 1 hour] - Task Beasts: Perform specific healing/soothing actions, then receive reward and leave. - Ally Support: Thank support and guide them away. - Normal Allies: Heal wounds.
LanguageData.Message_TITAN_VoidWalkerSafe: Void Walker confirms safety, returning to the Void.
LanguageData.Message_TITAN_SubjectRecruited: {0} sensed Prototype 0's resonance and willingly joined the colony!
LanguageData.Message_Spark_LethalStrikeDetected: Spark's core detected lethal blow! Emergency reorganization protocol initiated.
LanguageData.Message_Prototype_HibernationStarted: Prototype #0 sustained lethal damage! Self-preservation protocol initiated — forced hibernation engaged...
LanguageData.Message_Warlord_LeftData: War Trace has acknowledged you and left data.
LanguageData.Message_ThrumboMagnet_Attracted: Prototype #0's juvenile resonance attracted a distant herd.
LanguageData.Message_ThrumboMagnet_AlphaAttracted: Prototype #0's bloodline resonance stirred an Alpha Thrumbo — it's heading toward the colony.
LanguageData.Message_SpawnSpark_AlreadyHas: Signal invalid: That creature already seems to have one beside you.
LanguageData.Message_SpawnPrototype_AlreadyExists: Signal interference: Prototype #0 detected. As the only bio-weapon, only one can resonate at a time.
LanguageData.Letter_TitanReinforcement_Body: Sensing your crisis, {0} has come to aid you!
LanguageData.Letter_SparkSpawn_Body: As the scale releases special pheromone signals, a mutated boomrat with intense heat radiates into the map. It is "Spark", the closest friend of Prototype #0. It caught the familiar scent only it could recognize and came without hesitation. (Obtained: Spark - The immortal explosion expert)
LanguageData.Letter_PrototypeSpawn_Body: The incense burns down, and a tranquil scent that can calm berserk genes drifts into the wilderness. Soon, a golden figure appears at the map's edge—the Empire's lost test subject "#0". She looks exhausted, enduring constant pain from genetic modification. But now, your incense gives her first taste of peace in a long time. She tentatively approaches the colony, not as a weapon, but as a wanderer seeking shelter. (Obtained: [Prototype #0] - She trusts you and joins the colony)
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_EventHint: Faint resonance detected... awaiting first contact event.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No0_desc:
#0 is the beginning and end of Project TITAN. It is the only subject without an "offset module" — its gene architecture is the complete presentation of the original design. All subsequent derivatives were modifications offset from #0's template.

The Empire's Alien Biology Research Division originally envisioned creating a mass-producible biological weapon. #0's success proved the technology viable, but every subsequent "improvement" attempt ended in failure — No.1 dismantled #0's stability anchors, No.9 removed the neural insulation proteins in #0's golden fur, No.19 bypassed #0's termination signal, No.37 stacked unverified modules onto #0's architecture. Every failed record told the same story: #0 is not a toolkit that can be disassembled and reassembled — it is an organic whole.

#0 displayed a gentle and intelligent nature in the laboratory. It would remember the face of every researcher who came to feed it, and would gently touch the hand of anyone who seemed unhappy. One researcher said #0 had "an aura that makes you want to be better" — such observations were of course never recorded in rigorous scientific reports, but they were true.

#0's only "friend" was Spark — a strangely mutated boomrat. Spark was not a numbered test subject; it was spontaneously "adopted" by #0 in the lab. No one arranged for them to be together, and no one could explain why a high-level gene-modified thrumbo would be inseparable from a mutated rodent. But #0 would groom Spark's fur, and Spark would curl up quietly and fall asleep beside #0. This was the only relationship in Project TITAN never defined by any experimental protocol.

On the night of the lab explosion, #0 went missing in the chaos. Spark, tracking #0's scent, was also scattered. The story after that begins with a wisp of incense at a colony...

Features: Periodically sheds golden scales | Attracts wild thrumbos when adult (20% chance) | Active ability: Titan Resonance | Immortal (only falls down) | Fur glows a faint gold — the symbol of royal genes

[How to recruit] Craft and use Gene Incense to attract Prototype #0 to the colony.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_Spark_desc:
Spark is not an officially numbered test subject of Project TITAN. It was originally just a routine lab animal — a common boomrat used for toxicity testing. But it lived too close to #0's isolation chamber for too long.

No one can definitively explain how Spark's mutation occurred. The most likely explanation is that the psionic pulse released during #0's awakening accidentally rewrote Spark's gene expression — this kind of "radiation awakening" had been modeled in theory but never empirically observed. The result was Spark gaining an incredible ability: immortality. Whenever it suffers a fatal injury, its chemical fuel gland triggers a controlled detonation, destroying all close-range threats, then Spark enters a roughly 6-hour hibernation during which its cells reorganize at extreme speed. When it wakes, it's a perfectly fine boomrat again.

Spark's attachment to #0 defies biological explanation. It will不顾一切地 make its way to #0, even if firewalls, electrified fences, and sealed steel doors stand between them. Researchers discovered a special pheromone on #0's shed scales — a frequency only Spark's olfactory system can detect. #0 seems to actively release this signal, like a lighthouse lit for Spark.

After the lab explosion, Spark was the first individual confirmed to have escaped — its breakout method was simple: detonate itself, blast through rubble, hibernate for 6 hours, detonate again, until it found a way out. It detonated four times in the ruins before breaking free, and the scorch marks on its body have never fully faded.

Where Spark is now, no one knows. But if #0 is still alive, Spark will find her. It only needs the signal of a single scale.

Features: [Phoenix Protocol] Triggers massive explosion upon fatal injury and enters forced hibernation reboot | Soul bond with Prototype #0 | Produces chemfuel

[How to recruit] Obtain Prototype #0's shed scale and activate the signal. Spark will track it down.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No7_desc:
No.7 was a product of the second batch under the "progressive single-module offset" protocol. Its offset direction came from a military requirement — the Empire wanted to deploy biological weapons on tropical toxic-mist planets that could ignore environmental toxins. Researchers replaced #0's standard metabolic pathway with an alternative route based on salamander toxin-neutralizing proteins. This route lets No.7 directly convert environmental toxins into metabolic energy, at the cost of making its digestive system extremely aggressive — its body fluids, saliva, and even the surface of its horn are all covered in lethal mixed toxins.

No.7 was the first poison-type test subject to complete awakening and survive. Its mental state was stable — relatively speaking. Unlike #0's gentle nature, No.7 displayed marked territorial awareness and aggression, issuing low rumbling warnings to any creature that entered its sensory range. Researchers classified this as "defensive aggression," believing it was a side effect of the toxin metabolic pathway on the nervous system — the toxins constantly circulating within No.7 kept it in a state of "physiological-level vigilance."

Notably, No.7's attitude toward #0 was entirely different. Every time #0 was brought to the adjacent observation area, No.7 would quiet down, and those normally taut muscles would gradually relax. Researchers speculated this might be #0's "stability anchor effect" — #0's gene architecture might contain a bypass signal capable of soothing mutated subjects, and No.7 was especially sensitive to this signal.

When the lab exploded, No.7 was the third individual confirmed to have escaped. It burst straight through three tempered glass walls from its shattered isolation chamber — the toxins had corroded the silicon-oxygen bonds of the glass. The last footage captured showed No.7 disappearing deep into the forest, with vegetation withering and blackening in its footprints.

Features: HP 12.0 | Horn force 35 (Poison Bite) | Poison Bite 42 | Leg force 29 | Complete poison immunity | Immunity gain speed 1.05

[How to recruit] #0's resonance attracts No.7 — it charges into the colony map as a manhunter, but automatically calms down and joins the player faction when HP drops below 50% or it comes near #0.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No13_desc:
No.13 is the most dangerous derivative in the entire Project TITAN — this is not an assessment, this is a fact.

Its offset module is the sole surviving data legacy from the No.37 disaster. No.37's "combat instinct enhancement" module was torn to pieces in the stack collapse, but researchers extracted the core enhancement sequence from the surviving gene fragments, and then did what No.37's experimental team had not done — they installed only this one module, and strictly observed #0's termination signal protection protocol.

The result was both exhilarating and terrifying. No.13's aggressiveness far exceeded all expectations — its horn mutated into a serrated piercing structure, its tail evolved a bone blade resembling an axe, and its attack speed was 2.4 times #0's. Its immune system was also extremely enhanced for combat needs — almost no known pathogen could survive in its body for more than 12 hours. The price was that No.13's armor was far below #0's — the body tilted all resources toward offense, as if installing a pure killing engine inside a thrumbo's body.

But what unsettled researchers most was No.13's "gaze." The behavior of other test subjects could be explained by side effects of gene modification — No.7's vigilance, No.50's indifference, No.26's attachment — but No.13 was different. It would observe opponents' weaknesses while attacking, it would use terrain, it would feign retreat and then ambush. This was not instinct. This was tactics.

The research supervisor wrote a personal annotation on No.13's file: "I'm not sure whether we created a beast or a warrior. If the latter, I suggest we never find out what happens the day it decides we are also 'opponents.'"

On the night of the lab explosion, No.13's surveillance footage recorded a scene that sent chills down every reviewer's spine — 23 minutes before the explosion, No.13 stopped the pacing it had maintained for months, turned toward the observation window, and stared quietly at the camera, as if it knew someone was watching. Then it closed its eyes. After the explosion, No.13's isolation chamber was the only one opened from the inside.

Features: HP 13.0 | Horn force 34 (Piercing) | Tail strike 76 | Immune speed 3.5x | Low armor (Sharp 0.40, Blunt 0.25) — Glass cannon

[How to recruit] No.13 walks toward #0's position with battle scars, and immediately joins the player faction upon confirming #0's existence. Its goal is clear — it came for #0, not for a fight.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No26_desc:
No.26's birth originated from a non-military project — a mental health counselor among the Empire's researchers submitted a report noting that long-term contact with #0 significantly improved researchers' emotional states, and she hypothesized that #0 might possess some kind of "psychological soothing mechanism." Higher-ups scoffed at this, but approved the resource request — not out of care, but because "emotionally stable researchers are more efficient."

No.26's offset direction was the most unassuming string in #0's gene pool — the segments related to social behavior and parent-offspring attachment. Researchers assumed this was a trivial modification, but the result was No.26 becoming the most unexpected product of the entire Project TITAN.

It stopped growing during development, reaching an adult body size of only a large dog — this was an unexpected interaction between social behavior genes and #0's growth regulation sequence, causing growth hormones to be suppressed early. Its combat power was roughly equivalent to a husky's, its armor practically nonexistent, and its horn just a tiny protrusion. In military evaluation, No.26 was classified as "no battlefield value."

But No.26 had an ability no other test subject possessed — it made every creature that came into contact with it relax. Not just humans — even No.7 would stop its vigilance posture near No.26, and even No.13 would not attack No.26. Its filth rate was zero — its body seemed to naturally repel any dirt from adhering, as if even the earth couldn't bear to dirty it. Its wildness index was also zero — it would never initiate an attack on any creature, not even if it were hit.

Then, researchers noticed No.26's attitude toward #0. It wasn't No.7's "relaxes near #0," nor No.42's "maintains respect toward #0." No.26's feeling for #0 was a pure, unreserved attachment — it would follow #0 everywhere, sleep pressed against #0, and whimper anxiously when #0 left its sight. This emotion was so intense and pure that one researcher privately said: "If #0 is the sun, No.26 is the sunflower that always faces the sun."

#0's attitude toward No.26 was also different from other test subjects. #0 would actively groom No.26's fur, and tuck No.26 inside the curve of its body when resting. The first time researchers saw #0 display clear "protective" behavior was toward No.26.

When the lab exploded, No.26 and #0 were separated by the blast. Surveillance showed No.26 frantically running through the rubble, crying out something — acoustic analysis confirmed it was an imitation of #0's call; No.26 was calling for #0 using #0's own voice. After that, No.26 disappeared. But if #0 is still alive, No.26 will find her — this is the one thing everyone who knew No.26 was certain of. That amplified set of social genes endowed it with a near-supernatural sense of smell and tracking ability. For No.26, finding #0 is not a task — it is instinct, it is breathing, it is the entire reason for existence.

Features: HP 2.85 | Horn force 18 | Zero filth | Zero wildness | Never retaliates | High-frequency affection

[How to recruit] No.26 is #0's most loyal guardian — it walks straight to #0 and lies down beside #0, immediately joining the player faction. It is the first test subject to find #0.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No42_desc:
No.42's offset direction came from another of the Empire's strategic requirements — vanguard reconnaissance on frozen planets. Although #0 had exceptional adaptability, its cold resistance capped at around -65°C, making extended operations on deep-freeze planets impossible. Researchers extracted the base insulation sequence from #0's fur genes and superimposed antifreeze protein encoding from polar microorganisms.

The effect far exceeded expectations. No.42's fur restructured into a multi-layer system — the outer layer is a dense windproof and waterproof shell, the inner layer is a nano-scale aerogel insulation layer, and the space between is filled with polar antifreeze proteins. It can sleep peacefully at -150°C, a cold resistance limit even #0 cannot match. More surprisingly, its fur emits a faint aurora glow in darkness — researchers initially thought this was a genetic byproduct, only to discover it was No.42's active behavior. It can control the frequency and color of its luminescence, seemingly for some unknown form of communication.

No.42 is the most "quiet" presence among the test subjects — not silent, but possessed of a deep, almost philosophical stillness. It neither attacked researchers nor tried to please them. It simply stood there, watching, listening, as though comprehending something far beyond the laboratory's score. One researcher said that being near No.42 made them involuntarily contemplate the boundaries of the universe and the nature of time, and then be frightened by such thoughts and quickly leave.

Notably, No.42 is the only test subject that refuses to eat trees. #0 would occasionally gnaw on bark — a thrumbo's natural instinct. But No.42 would carefully detour around every tree, even gently touching a trunk with its nose tip after accidentally brushing against one, as if apologizing. Researchers still cannot explain this behavior — the polar adaptation module contained no relevant instructions.

No.42's behavior on the night of the lab explosion was also unlike any other. While other test subjects escaped — running, smashing, corroding — No.42 simply raised its head, looked at a patch of starry sky revealed through the ruptured ceiling, and then walked slowly out of its isolation chamber. Surveillance captured the image of it gazing up at the stars amidst the explosion's firelight — privately called "the three most beautiful seconds of Project TITAN" by researchers.

It walked into the nearest snowfield, and there have been no confirmed sightings since. But if #0's signal is strong enough, perhaps the aurora will appear again.

Features: HP 12.0 | Horn force 53 | Extreme cold resistance (-150°C) | Low filth rate | Does not eat trees | Can cross-breed (requires Odyssey DLC)

[How to recruit] No.42 approaches #0 suffering from Genetic Drift (TITAN_GeneticDrift) — it needs #0's resonance to cure its genetic defect. Use Titan Resonance to treat it, each use reducing severity by 35% until fully cured.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No45_desc:
No.45's offset direction originated from an unexpected discovery — during routine thermal imaging scans of #0, researchers noticed that #0's body temperature would briefly spike to three times its normal value during sleep, lasting about 45 seconds before falling back. This was confirmed to be part of #0's self-repair mechanism — the localized high temperature accelerated cell division and protein folding. But what if this thermal metabolism could be sustained continuously, not just limited to repair periods?

No.45's research team extracted the regulatory genes from #0's thermal pulse sequence and linked them to a master promoter with continuous rather than pulsed expression. The result was a living thermal engine — No.45's core temperature is constant at around 230°C, and even its surface temperature reaches 80°C. Its fur contains asbestos-like mineralized fibers, making it nearly incombustible. Its horn glows a faint dark red under high heat, like a red-hot iron skewer.

But the most unexpected byproduct was chemfuel. No.45's high-temperature metabolism incompletely breaks down organic matter from its food, discharging liquid hydrocarbons through its mammary glands — this is the source of its chemfuel production. About 18 units every 3 days is double that of similar test subjects, and considering No.45 also possesses combat capability alongside fuel output, its "cost-effectiveness" earned military approval.

No.19's lesson was strictly observed — No.45's regeneration system retained #0's complete termination signal, and additionally incorporated an "overheat circuit breaker" mechanism: if body temperature exceeds the safety threshold, metabolism automatically downshifts into cooling mode. This design proved successful — No.45 never experienced the kind of regeneration runaway that No.19 suffered.

No.45's personality was surprisingly gentle — researchers called it a "hearth-fire personality." It was not aggressive, not aloof, just quietly radiating stable warmth. One researcher reported feeling a hearth-like peace when approaching No.45's isolation chamber in winter. No.45 did indeed actively approach other test subjects in cold areas — not to attack, but purely to let them warm up a bit.

When the lab exploded, No.45's isolation chamber was melted through — not blasted open, but No.45 pushed its body temperature to the limit and burned a hole through the steel wall. Surveillance showed its fur flickering with incandescent light as it walked out of the rubble, like a walking furnace. Then in the night, that glow gradually receded, grew smaller, and finally disappeared over the horizon — like a shooting star fallen into the wilderness.

Features: HP 12.0 | Horn force 36 | Produces chemfuel: 18/3 days | Nearly incombustible | Heat armor 1.00

[How to recruit] No.45 walks out of the fire near the colony, engulfed in flames yet completely unharmed, and immediately joins the player faction.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No50_desc:
No.50's offset direction came from a seemingly simple military requirement — logistical self-sufficiency. The Empire's biological weapons needed to operate independently for extended periods without resupply on battlefields, and #0's food intake (1.75 times a normal thrumbo's) was a clear disadvantage in resource-scarce areas. Researchers replaced #0's metabolic regulation sequence with a camel-style throttling mode, suppressing the hunger rate to 0.25 — less than a third of #0's.

This modification was physiologically successful, but produced behavioral side effects the researchers hadn't anticipated. No.50's "stubbornness" was not due to strong wildness — quite the opposite. It was simply extremely obstinate. A normal thrumbo has a 10% chance of retaliating when taming fails; No.50 raised this to 17% — not out of anger, but because it simply refused to stop whatever it was doing. You can't call it away when it's grazing, you can't stop it when it's walking, you can't wake it when it's resting. It's not fighting you — it just doesn't care about you.

This stubbornness extended to No.50's attitude toward survival. Researchers once conducted an extreme starvation experiment — 30 consecutive days without feeding No.50. #0 under the same conditions began wasting away by day 8, while No.50 had only lost 7% of its body weight by day 30, with all physiological indicators still normal. When researchers finally provided food, No.50 ambled over at its own pace, unhurriedly ate a few bites, then found a spot to continue sleeping — as if the past 30 days were just another afternoon in its routine.

Evaluations of No.50 were deeply divided. The military deemed it "uncontrollable" and advised against deployment. Logistics considered its self-sustainability a "strategic-level advantage." In the end, neither side got what they wanted — No.50 just continued doing what it had always done: standing there, slowly digesting, waiting for the next moment that didn't require its response.

When the lab exploded, No.50 was the last to leave — not because it couldn't escape, but because it took a long time to decide this matter was worth getting up from its nap for. Surveillance showed that 27 minutes after the explosion, No.50 finally and unhurriedly walked into the desert. It didn't even look back at the burning laboratory.

Features: HP 12.0 | Horn force 35 | Hunger rate 0.25 | Heat tolerance (75°C) | Tame-fail retaliation 17%

[How to recruit] No.50 slowly walks toward #0's position, then lies down beside #0 and immediately joins the player faction. It doesn't care about you — it just chooses to stay near #0.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No64_desc:
No.64's offset direction was another answer to the question of "how to make #0 harder to kill." No.19 chose regeneration enhancement — which failed by breaching #0's termination signal. No.64's team chose a fundamentally different route: don't repair faster — just don't get injured in the first place.

They extracted hardening sequences from #0's bone and keratin mineralization genes and increased their expression fourfold. The result was No.64's entire skeletal system and surface covering undergoing drastic mineralization remodeling — its horn became a massive bone dome, subcutaneous bone plates reached 3.2 times #0's density, and sharp armor reached a staggering 0.95 (approaching the protection level of solid steel plate). It even formed a hemispherical bone dome at the front of its skull — researchers initially thought it was a tumor, but later discovered it was a perfect energy-absorbing structure that could distribute blunt impacts across the entire cranium.

The cost was obvious. No.64 was the slowest of all surviving test subjects — move speed 4.6, only slightly faster than a human. It was also the only test subject with a health coefficient lower than #0's (6.0 vs #0's baseline), because the super-mineralized skeleton appropriated so much body weight allocation that the body could only cut organ redundancy to maintain balance. But No.64 hardly needed organ redundancy — it simply never got injured to a degree that required redundancy. Its pain threshold was calibrated to 0.87 — meaning it had to sustain near-lethal damage before being incapacitated by pain. For a beast that's nearly impossible to hurt, this was hardly a drawback.

No.64's personality was so composed it seemed almost dull — but this was misleading. Researchers once considered No.64 the "least intelligent" test subject, until during one test, No.64 did something shocking when attacked: it crouched down. The super-mineralized dome pointed toward the attack source, limbs tucked beneath its body, forming an almost impregnable defensive posture. This was not an instinctive reaction — No.64 evaluated the attack angle, its own vulnerability distribution, and optimal defensive posture within 3 seconds, then executed a calculated tactical action.

No.13 is kill instinct. No.64 is survival instinct. They are two sides of the same coin from #0's gene pool.

When the lab exploded, No.64 was the only test subject that didn't need to flee. Debris and shockwaves hit it like rain on stone. It stood for a long time, confirmed there were no further threats, and then walked into the grassland with its characteristically slow and deliberate stride. No predator along the way dared to obstruct it — not out of fear, but because instinct told them this moving wall was not worth attempting.

Features: HP 12.0 | Dome force 39 | Sharp armor 0.95 | Blunt armor 0.75 | Heat armor 0.55 | Pain threshold 0.95 | Move speed 4.6

[How to recruit] No.64 appears near the colony while being pursued by pirates — use Titan Resonance or wait for the pirates to leave. No.64 will join the player faction after confirming safety.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No88_desc:
No.88's offset direction was the most controversial in Project TITAN — introducing plant genes into a thrumbo's metabolic system. The proposer was confronted at the approval meeting: "Are you making a beast or a tree?" His answer: "I'm making a beast that can feed a colony."

No.88's modification was based on a set of symbiotic microorganism genes discovered in #0's fur — #0's periodically shed golden scales were covered in a special micro-ecological environment, and these microorganisms had formed a mutualistic relationship with #0. No.88's team took this concept to the extreme: instead of cultivating microorganisms on fur, they directly embedded gene-edited symbiotic fungal strains into No.88's subcutaneous tissue.

These fungi are connected to No.88's circulatory system, drawing basic nutrients from the blood while delivering synthesized products — the active compounds of medicinal herbs — through the lymphatic system to No.88's body surface. Approximately every 4 days, specific glands on No.88 secrete 4 units of herbal medicine — researchers jokingly called No.88 a "walking apothecary garden." No.88 itself also benefited: the symbiotic fungi provided some immune enhancement, though far from No.13's extreme level, it was enough to keep No.88 healthy in disease-ridden jungle environments.

The cost was that No.88 gained a deadly weakness of plants — fear of fire. #0's flammability is close to normal, while No.88's reaches 1.2 — it burns more easily than ordinary creatures. The symbiotic fungi's mycelium network rapidly dies under high temperatures while releasing pro-combustion volatile substances, causing fire to burn ever more fiercely on No.88. This was a problem the team discovered early in the experiment, but they chose to retain this weakness — because removing the symbiotic fungi would simultaneously remove all of No.88's production capabilities.

No.88's personality was unexpectedly warm — more outgoing and proactive than No.26. No.26's attachment was directed solely at #0, while No.88 showed friendliness to all creatures that approached it. It would actively nuzzle visitors' hands and emit soft purring sounds. Researchers believed this was the symbiotic fungi's indirect influence on the nervous system — No.88 was like a sunflower, instinctively opening itself toward every direction that might "spread spores."

The lab explosion was a nightmare for No.88 — fire was everywhere, and for it, that was worse than death. Surveillance showed No.88 letting out a tearing scream the instant the explosion occurred. Its fur went from calm green to scorched withered yellow in 3 seconds. Unlike the other test subjects that headed outdoors, it charged toward the nearest water source and plunged headfirst into the cooling pool, staying submerged until the flames subsided, then climbed out covered in steam and squeezed out through a broken water pipe.

To this day, No.88's burn scars are still faintly visible. Those areas will never grow fungi again, nor produce herbal medicine. But No.88 still chooses to approach colonists sitting by campfires — not for warmth, but because it remembers that in the laboratory, the people who talked to it always sat beside the fire.

Features: HP 12.0 | Horn force 35 | Produces herbal medicine: 4/4 days | Flammability 1.2 (fatal fire weakness) | Low wildness

[How to recruit] No.88 flees from fire with burn injuries and heads toward the nearest water source. It joins the player faction after being healed.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No1_desc:
#0's success plunged the entire research division into a dangerous euphoria. The Empire's leadership finally saw hope — a stable, reproducible gene-modified thrumbo living subject. But #0 was too special, with too many stacked gene modules, making it impossible for researchers to determine which module played the critical role with which other module. Thus, the "disassembly and reconstruction" plan was launched.

No.1 was the first "simplified blueprint." Researchers stripped away from #0's gene architecture everything they considered the core skeleton and removed all "redundant" modules. No.1 developed perfectly in the culture fluid through the 72nd hour — its cell division curve was even smoother than #0's, unburdened by those "interference items."

At dawn on day 4, No.1's cells began differentiating out of control. Not cancer, but an exposure of the fundamental error the researchers had made — those gene fragments labeled "redundant" were actually stability anchors in #0's gene architecture. Every module stacked on #0 was mutually bound and mutually constrained, together forming the precision balance of a self-stabilizing system. No.1 had removed the critical fulcrum of this balance.

Genes from different species each "awoke" within No.1, attempting to independently construct separate organ systems. In 6 hours, its body simultaneously grew incomplete hooves, claws, and scales, while bones fractured and reassembled in three different directions. Finally it dissolved into a murky pool of tissue fluid.

The research supervisor wrote at the end of the report: "#0 is not a toolkit that can be disassembled and reassembled — it is an organic whole. Any simplification of #0's gene architecture must reassess its cascade effects." Thereafter, "progressive single-module offset" replaced "disassembly and reconstruction" as the new research policy.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No9_desc:
Based on long-term observation of #0, researchers noticed a peculiar phenomenon — after awakening, #0 displayed neural activity far exceeding expectations, but its cortical signal patterns matched no known organism. The research team classified this anomaly as the "psionic threshold": during development, #0 completed some kind of whole-brain synaptic rewiring, similar to a butterfly emerging from its chrysalis.

No.9 was the first attempt to reproduce this rewiring in a non-prototype subject. Researchers selected the embryo closest in development to #0 — gene scans were nearly identical, the only difference being the removal of golden fur-related expression genes. Theoretically, No.9 should have perfectly replicated #0's awakening pathway.

Rewiring stopped at 87%.

Surveillance records showed that at the moment rewiring was interrupted, No.9 opened its eyes. Its pupils rapidly dilated then contracted, and a low, rhythmic vibration came from its mouth — acoustic analysis confirmed this was a thrumbo distress frequency, but distorted into a variation never heard before. Brainwaves showed its consciousness briefly "lit up" — it knew where it was, knew what it was, knew what had gone wrong — and then went dark forever.

Post-analysis indicated that #0's rewiring succeeded because its golden fur genes — that string of "redundancy" removed from No.9 — actually encoded a special neural insulation protein. Without this insulation layer, No.9's psionic signals suffered a catastrophic short circuit during the rewiring process.

No.9's body lived for another 11 days, organ functions perfectly normal. But no signal ever came from those eyes again. The diagnosis was "irreversible brain death," but privately some admitted it seemed more like a kind of "voluntary shutdown" — as if something at the last moment had seen through the nature of this laboratory and chosen not to come back.

No.9's eyes were preserved in the low-temperature sample repository.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No19_desc:
#0's greatest military value lay in its near-immortal self-repair capability. The Empire urgently wanted to replicate this ability in other test subjects, and even enhance it further — a self-healing weapon is a good weapon, but an instantly self-healing weapon is the perfect weapon.

The research team extracted the core sequences from #0's repair mechanism and superimposed a reinforcement module adapted from salamander regeneration genes. Theoretically, No.19 should repair faster and more thoroughly than #0. Gene architecture review passed — all modules had precedent validation, and there was no disassembly risk like No.1.

No.19 was the first derivative test subject to cross the "psionic threshold" and complete awakening. Initial results were stunning — a routine sampling skin wound healed completely in 4 minutes with no scar. #0's equivalent wound took 22 minutes. The data was exhilarating.

But the regeneration mechanism produced an "over-interpretation." #0's self-repair system has a precise "termination signal" — when tissue recovers to its original state, the repair process automatically stops. No.19's enhancement module bypassed this termination signal. Its body began treating all "less than perfect" tissue as damage requiring repair — old scars were dissolved and regrown, and the new tissue was then judged as "insufficiently mature" and dissolved again — an endless cycle of reconstruction.

By day 23, regeneration hotspots had spread across 40% of No.19's body surface. Tissue visibly swelled, dissolved, and swelled again. Bones became deformed and brittle from repeated remodeling. But No.19 remained conscious throughout — it sensed what was happening inside itself, its eyes holding pure understanding and silent endurance.

The euthanasia decision was made on day 24. During execution, No.19 did not struggle, and even leaned its head toward the syringe of its own accord.

Thereafter, the "termination signal" in #0's gene architecture was listed as the highest-priority protection target — no regeneration-class modification was permitted to touch or bypass this mechanism.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No37_desc:
No.37 emerged during Project TITAN's most anxious period. The Empire's patience had run out — they had the perfect specimen in #0, yet couldn't mass-produce any usable derivative. Every departure from #0's template ended in failure. Leadership issued an ultimatum: produce a deployable variant within three months, or the entire project would be restructured.

The research team was under unprecedented pressure. No.1's cautionary tale still loomed — disassembly and reconstruction meant certain death. Progressive single-module offset was too slow; three months was nowhere near enough. Ultimately, someone proposed a compromise: no longer disassemble #0, nor offset step by step, but simultaneously stack multiple verified modules onto #0's complete architecture.

No.37 had three modules stacked simultaneously: toxin neutralization, extreme climate adaptation, and the never-tested-on-a-living-body "combat instinct enhancement." The first two performed perfectly in standalone testing; the third had only passed petri dish validation. The interactive effects of all three had never been evaluated — there was no time.

No.37 developed normally — too normally. Scans showed its gene chains stable as a textbook, three modules coexisting harmoniously without any rejection signals. Researchers breathed sighs of relief and began drafting field deployment plans.

On day 301, without warning, No.37's chromosome 7 fractured at the short arm of region 4. Gene chains unraveled like a zipper torn apart by invisible hands from the break point cascading toward both ends. Gene fragments from all three modules mixed in, undergoing catastrophic disorderly recombination with #0's template genome.

From the first fracture to No.37's entire genetic information becoming unordered base fragments — the entire process took only 47 seconds.

No.37's remains looked exactly as they had in life — glossy fur, beautiful physique, as if merely sleeping peacefully. But every cell had become a meaningless organic mixture.

The No.37 incident led to a complete restructuring of the research team. The new supervisor abolished all stacking protocols, strictly requiring adherence to #0's single-module progressive procedure. He also did something his predecessor had never done — he walked into #0's enclosure himself and sat beside it for an entire afternoon. That night's log contained only one sentence: "We have been disassembling #0 to build new weapons. But perhaps #0 was never meant to be taken apart."

Three months later, Project TITAN's laboratory was destroyed in an explosion of unknown cause. Prototype #0 went missing.
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_No7_Desc: A thrumbo covered in corrosive venom has entered the map as a manhunter! But when severely wounded or near Prototype #0, it seems to calm down...
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_No13_Desc: A battle-scarred thrumbo walks directly toward Prototype #0. Its eyes hold no hostility — only a clear destination. It immediately joins your faction.
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_No26_Desc: A dog-sized thrumbo appears at the map edge. It ignores everything and runs straight to #0, then lies down quietly beside #0. No.26 has joined the colony.
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_No42_Desc: An aurora-glowing thrumbo slowly approaches #0's position. It suffers from Genetic Drift — use Titan Resonance to cure it (each use reduces severity by 35%).
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_No45_Desc: A small fire erupts at the map edge — then a thrumbo walks out of the flames, completely unharmed! No.45 has joined the colony.
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_No50_Desc: A thrumbo slowly walks into the colony, ignoring all the commotion. It walks straight to #0 and lies down. No.50 has joined the colony.
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_No64_Desc: A giant thrumbo is being chased by pirates! Attacks bounce off it like rain on stone. After the pirates leave, No.64 will confirm safety and join your faction.
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_No88_Desc: A burn-covered thrumbo frantically runs into the colony! It's heading for the nearest water source — it's terrified of fire. No.88 will join after recovery.
LanguageData.TITAN_Message_ExperimentIncompatibleMuta: {0}'s gene architecture cannot accept this mutagen type.
LanguageData.TITAN_Message_No42_DriftHealed: No.42's Genetic Drift has been fully cured!
LanguageData.TITAN_Message_No42_DriftHealing: Treating No.42's Genetic Drift... Current severity: {0}
LanguageData.TITAN_Message_No7_SelfTamed: {0} has calmed down under #0's resonance and joined the colony!
LanguageData.TITAN_Settings_CompanionChance_NTRequire: Companion Chance: Requires New Thrumbos! mod
LanguageData.TITAN_Settings_AlphaReplaceChance: Alpha Thrumbo Replace Chance: {0} (Requires Odyssey DLC)
LanguageData.TITAN_Settings_AlphaReplaceChance_Odysse: Alpha Thrumbo Replace Chance: Requires Odyssey DLC
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_ResetWarning: Warning: Reset will clear all discovery records. This cannot be undone.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No0_hint: The ultimate product of Project TITAN, possessing pure royal bloodline.\n\n[How to recruit] Craft and use Gene Incense to attract Prototype #0 to your colony.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_Spark_hint: Prototype #0's only friend — a mutated boomrat with immortality.\n\n[How to recruit] Obtain Prototype #0's shed scale, then activate the signal. Spark will track it down.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_Warlord_hint: Legendary ancient beast, berserk melee overlord.\n\n[How to recruit] When the Warlord event triggers, use Titan Resonance to soothe its rage. After calming, gain Crimson Keratin data core.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_VoidWalker_hint: Legendary ancient beast, hunted phase-shifting master.\n\n[How to recruit] When the Void Walker event triggers, clear all enemies, then use Titan Resonance to guide it to safety. Gain Void Dust phase data.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_Matriarch_hint: Legendary ancient beast, suffering from genetic collapse.\n\n[How to recruit] When the Matriarch event triggers, use Titan Resonance repeatedly to cure its genetic collapse. Gain Life Resin living sample.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_Warlord_desc:
One of the Three Titans. A semi-finished product from the "Prototype Era" of Project TITAN in ancient times — unlike the later numbered test subjects, War Trace was a product from before the Empire's strict controls, its gene architecture retaining the raw violence sequences from before #0's creation.

It is enormous in size. Though not agile in movement, every strike carries devastating force. It radiates "Blood Rage" psionic waves that can drive all nearby creatures into a berserk state. Its fur bears dark red patterns, like bloodstains that can never be washed clean.

War Trace will not actively attack #0 — it seems to recognize some ancient authority upon #0. But everything else, including colonists, can be caught in its fury.

Researchers speculate that War Trace was a "reject" produced in the same batch as #0's awakening — its psionic circuitry was too crude for precise regulation like #0's, instead continuously radiating outward in an uncontrolled manner. This makes it a walking buff tower on the battlefield — friend and foe alike receive enhancement.

[How to befriend] Use Prototype #0's [Titan Resonance] ability on it. Each soothing accumulates 1 layer of resonance; reaching 3 layers calms its rage. It will acknowledge you and leave Crimson Keratin peacefully.

[Drop] Crimson Keratin (Data Core) — Analyze to unlock Crimson Mutagen blueprint.

[Special] May airdrop to assist during colony raids.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_VoidWalker_desc:
One of the Three Titans. A phase-shifting master hunted by unknown forces — its gene architecture contains phase-shift technology the Empire spent decades decrypting, and its pursuers clearly don't want it falling into anyone's hands.

Void Walker's body is covered in a semi-transparent phase field, giving its silhouette a constant "not quite in reality" blurriness. Its dodge ability is extraordinarily high, almost able to predict and deflect the vast majority of melee and ranged attacks. It radiates "Void Veil" psionic waves that grant dodge bonuses to nearby allies.

It doesn't want to stay long — whenever threats appear nearby, it attempts to phase-shift away; only after confirming absolute safety will it communicate with #0.

Void Walker is the most "rational" of the Three Titans. It is not berserk like War Trace, nor gentle like Matriarch of Life. It simply calmly assesses the situation and makes the optimal choice. Some speculate that its phase abilities allowed it to see something "that should not have been seen," which is what led to its forced flight.

[How to befriend] Clear all hostile units from the map, then use Prototype #0's [Titan Resonance] to communicate with it. After confirming safety, it leaves Void Dust and returns to the void.

[Drop] Void Dust (Phase Data) — Analyze to unlock Void Mutagen blueprint.

[Special] May airdrop to assist during colony raids.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_Matriarch_desc:
One of the Three Titans. A behemoth afflicted with genetic collapse — it is the oldest of the Three Titans, and the one most deeply tormented by Project TITAN's aftereffects. Its self-repair system, though powerful, hovers on the edge of total failure, the body constantly cycling between regeneration and collapse.

Matriarch of Life radiates "Life Bloom" psionic waves that accelerate wound healing for all nearby creatures — a side effect of its runaway regenerative energy leaking outward in radiation form. Its body is three times the size of a normal thrumbo, its fur displaying a strange texture of interwoven withering and flourishing — half vivid green of new growth, half withered yellow of decay.

It is the gentlest of the Three Titans. Even in agonizing pain, it never initiates an attack on any creature. When it approaches the colony, colonists can feel a strange sense of peace — its psionics inadvertently heal everything around it. Even in collapse, it still gives.

#0 has a special reaction to Matriarch of Life — compared to its vigilance toward War Trace and curiosity toward Void Walker, #0 displays clear "concern" for the Matriarch. Researchers hypothesize that Matriarch of Life was #0's earliest "companion" during awakening, and its presence gave #0 the first warmth it ever knew.

[How to befriend] Use Prototype #0's [Titan Resonance] multiple times to treat it. Each resonance cures a portion of genetic collapse, requiring repeated treatments until full recovery. After being cured, it leaves Life Resin and departs in peace.

[Drop] Life Resin (Living Sample) — Analyze to unlock Verdant Mutagen blueprint.

[Special] May airdrop to assist during colony raids.
LanguageData.TITAN_Gizmo_ViewCodexDesc: View this subject's detailed Titan Codex information
LanguageData.TITAN_HerdAchievement_Tier1: When the 10th thrumbo entered the colony, Prototype #0 let out a long, resonant call. All companions seemed to become more alert and confident.\n\n[Effect] All thrumbo-kind gain "Herd Resonance — Glimmer": melee damage +10%, move speed +5%. All colonists gain mood buff "All Creatures Come — Dawn".
LanguageData.TITAN_HerdAchievement_Tier2: The 20th thrumbo has arrived. Prototype #0's call grows deeper and more powerful. Companions seem to have formed an invisible network of awareness.\n\n[Effect] Upgraded to "Herd Resonance — Spark": melee damage +20%, move speed +10%, armor +10%. All colonists gain mood buff "All Creatures Come — Zenith".
LanguageData.TITAN_HerdAchievement_Tier3: 50 thrumbos — the colony has become a true thrumbo sanctuary. Prototype #0 unleashes an earth-shaking roar, and the entire colony feels that ancient majesty.\n\n[Effect] Upgraded to "Herd Resonance — Dawn": melee damage +30%, move speed +15%, armor +20%, regeneration +0.5/day. All colonists gain mood buff "All Creatures Come — Apex".
LanguageData.TITAN_Ability_ResonanceDesc:
Channel Prototype #0's royal genes to release a resonance wave.
- Quest Titans: Perform specific healing/soothing operations, then receive reward and depart.
- Allied units: Thank them for support and guide them away.
- Normal allies: Heal wounds.
LanguageData.TITAN_Ability_Tier1Bonus: [Resonance Amplification] Range +50%, heal amount +50%, cooldown -25%. Passive: resonance pulse (8-tile range, slowly heals all friendly units)
LanguageData.TITAN_Ability_Tier2Bonus: [Sync Pulse] Passive resonance pulse range expands to 12 tiles
LanguageData.TITAN_Ability_Tier3Bonus: [Progenitor Echo] Passive resonance pulse range expands to 15 tiles; active resonance also heals all friendly units within range
LanguageData.TITAN_Ability_Tier4Bonus: [Royal Awakening] Passive resonance pulse range expands to 20 tiles; unlocks Royal Mark
LanguageData.TITAN_Ability_RoyalMarkDesc: Brand a target with #0's royal bloodline, permanently +15% all stats. Only works on thrumbo-kind.
LanguageData.TITAN_Message_ProgenitorEcho: Progenitor Echo: {0} friendly units received resonance healing
LanguageData.TITAN_Message_RoyalMark_NotThrumboKind: Target is not thrumbo-kind — Royal Mark cannot be applied
LanguageData.TITAN_Message_RoyalMark_AlreadyMarked: Target already has a Royal Mark
LanguageData.TITAN_Message_RoyalMark_Applied: {0} has been marked with the Royal Mark — all stats +15%
LanguageData.TITAN_Resonance_Tier1Unlocked: [Resonance Amplification] Prototype #0 has formed resonance links with 5 thrumbo-kind! Titan Resonance range, healing, and cooldown are enhanced. Passive resonance pulse activates (8-tile range, slowly heals all friendly units).
LanguageData.TITAN_Resonance_Tier2Unlocked: [Sync Pulse] Prototype #0's resonance with 15 thrumbo-kind deepens! Passive resonance pulse range expands to 12 tiles.
LanguageData.TITAN_Resonance_Tier3Unlocked: [Progenitor Echo] Prototype #0's resonance with 30 thrumbo-kind reaches its peak! Passive resonance pulse range expands to 15 tiles; active resonance also heals all friendly units within range.
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_HunterAttack_Desc:
The Empire's detectors have locked onto Prototype #0's energy signal.

A pack of gene-modified "Hunters" has arrived in the area. Their brains have been implanted with absolute directives — they will tear through anything standing between them and their target.

Protect #0!
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No7_hint: The air reeks of strong acidic corrosion. Prototype #0's resonance system has detected an unstable high-acid metabolism signal...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No13_hint: Deep growls echo from the swamp. A creature bearing deep wounds is approaching #0's location...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No26_hint: The resonance radar captures a faint but very close signal — something small is tracking Prototype #0's scent...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No42_hint: Aurora-like flickers in the distance. An individual with extremely unstable gene sequences is drifting somewhere...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No45_hint: Anomalous heat source detected. Core temperature far exceeds normal values...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No50_hint: The resonance signal from the desert is unusually weak yet persistent. An individual that seems capable of going long periods without food is wandering...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No64_hint: Super-mineralized bio-shell signal detected. Its bone hardness approaches pure steel plate...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No88_hint: Painful cries come from the direction of the fire. A burn-wounded individual is searching for water...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No1_hint: Fragments of early experiment records remain in the archive... Terminated.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No9_hint: Residual brainwave signals indicate this individual's consciousness has completely dissipated...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No19_hint: The frozen sample vial bears the number 19... Long beyond waking.
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No37_hint: The cultivation pod's last log entry is beyond reckoning...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No3_hint: Partial data encrypted by Imperial security protocol. Clearance code: Abyss...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No11_hint: Partial data encrypted by Imperial security protocol. Clearance code: Rift...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No15_hint: Partial data encrypted by Imperial security protocol. Clearance code: Shard...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No33_hint: Partial data encrypted by Imperial security protocol. Clearance code: Echo...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No55_hint: Partial data encrypted by Imperial security protocol. Clearance code: Relic...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No77_hint: Partial data encrypted by Imperial security protocol. Clearance code: Ember...
LanguageData.TITAN_Codex_No99_hint: Partial data encrypted by Imperial security protocol. Clearance code: Omega...
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_VoidWalkerRescue_Desc:
An injured "Void Walker" has fled into the area. Its body is covered with wounds caused by Imperial-standard weapons.

A group of [mercenaries disguised as Imperial troops] has been detected hunting it.

The Void Walker is heading toward your base for shelter! Protect it!
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_SpawnSpark_Desc:
The scale emits a special pheromone signal, and a heavily heated mutated boomrat charges into the map.

It is "Spark", Prototype #0's only friend. It smelled the familiar scent that only it can identify, and came without hesitation.

(Obtained: Spark - The infinitely respawning explosive expert)
LanguageData.TITAN_Letter_SpawnPrototype_Desc:
The incense burns out, and a calming scent that soothes violent genes drifts into the wilderness.

Soon, a golden figure appears at the map edge — it is the Empire's lost experiment subject "Prototype #0".

She looks exhausted, long suffering from the pain of genetic modification. But now, your incense has given her a long-overdue sense of peace.

She timidly approaches the colony — not as a weapon, but as a wanderer seeking shelter.

(Obtained: [Prototype #0] - She trusts you and has joined the colony)
LanguageData.Message_Titan_Vanished: {0}'s power has returned to the void, leaving only a fading silhouette.
LanguageData.TITAN_Gizmo_DismissTitanDesc: Send this titan back to the void early.
LanguageData.Message_Titan_Dismissed: {0} honors your wish and returns to the void.
